description: The map displays various land cover/land use polygons with numerical labels, overlaid with linear features, and an "Identify" tool pop-up. The dominant land cover is represented by light green polygons, with specific areas labelled with codes such as `1111`, `1112`, `1113`, `1211`, `2111`, `4111`, and `4112`, likely referring to a CORINE Land Cover (CLC) or similar classification scheme. An elongated, irregularly shaped polygon, also labelled `1111`, is highlighted in light purple, indicating it has been selected. Linear features, possibly roads or paths, are shown as thin grey lines separating some polygons. A bold red line marks a boundary on the right side of the map, consistent with a riparian zone (RZ) Area of Interest (AoI) border mentioned in the surrounding text. A GIS "Identify" pop-up window is visible in the lower right, stating "Identify from: <Top-most layer>", a "Location" coordinate of "4.228.487,895 2.941.473,325 Meters", and "Identified 1 feature". The map illustrates a Minimum Mapping Unit (MMU) exception rule for objects located at the border of a riparian zone.
